Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Context PostgreSQL database with tables claims adjusters claim_events Process R A E P N C Temporal Profile Model Provided as a dictionary with AVG STDEV for pairs Anomalies Highlighted in Model R to P Avg 25h STDEV 1h Low STDEV rigid timing P to N Avg 7 days STDEV 2 days Long delay high variance A to C Avg 2h STDEV 1h Too fast might skip steps E to N Avg 5 min STDEV 1 min Too fast might skip steps Tasks Identify anomalies Generate hypotheses Propose SQL verification queries Constraint Do not reference instructions explanations Present independently

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: R to P Average 90000 seconds 1 day STDEV 3600 seconds 1 hour Analysis The process usually goes R A E P If R to P is 1 day and R to E is 1 day and R to A is 1 hour this implies E to P is almost instantaneous or the timing is locked The STDEV is very low compared to the mean This suggests a fixed delay or a system constraint forcing P to happen exactly 25 hours after R regardless of intermediate steps

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: P to N Average 604800 seconds 7 days STDEV 172800 seconds 2 days Analysis 7 days is a long time to wait between Approval and Notification High STDEV means inconsistency Some might be fast some slow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A to C Average 7200 seconds 2 hours STDEV 3600 seconds 1 hour Analysis A is Assign C is Close Normal flow is A E P N C If A to C is only 2 hours this is impossibly fast for a full process It suggests claims are closing right after assignment skipping E P N This strongly suggests skipping steps or erroneous data

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: E to N Average 300 seconds 5 mins STDEV 60 seconds 1 min Analysis E is Evaluate N is Notify Normal flow is E P N If E to N is 5 mins P is likely skipped or instantaneous 5 mins is very fast for a human to evaluate and then notify especially if P is involved Low STDEV suggests automation or a rigid skip

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: R to P Anomaly Hypothesis 1 Automated approval rule triggers exactly 24 hours after submission for specific claim types Hypothesis 2 System bug where timestamp for P is set to submission date plus 1 day if not updated Hypothesis 3 Approve activity is logged automatically by a cron job regardless of actual evaluation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: P to N Anomaly Hypothesis 1 Notification batch process runs weekly causing a lag up to 7 days Hypothesis 2 High volume of approvals overwhelms the notification queue Hypothesis 3 Manual review required before notification causing delays and variance

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: A to C Anomaly Hypothesis 1 Claims are being rejected or closed immediately after assignment due to fraud detection or invalid data Hypothesis 2 Data entry error where Close is logged prematurely Hypothesis 3 Specific adjusters have a fast track or immediate deny workflow

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: E to N Anomaly Hypothesis 1 Evaluation triggers an automated notification script instantly skipping the formal Approve step in the log Hypothesis 2 Notify is actually a system status update that happens right after evaluation not a customer notification Hypothesis 3 Process bypass where E leads directly to N for low value claims

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Goal Verify hypotheses using claim_events claims adjusters Query Structure Need to join claim_events to get pairs calculate time differences and filter Key SQL Patterns Self join on claim_events for pairs EXTRACT EPOCH FROM for seconds Window functions or LAG LEAD if checking sequence but pairs are safer with self join for arbitrary pairs Join with claims for claim_type claim_amount Join with adjusters via resource

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 1 R to P Rigid Timing Find claims where P is exactly 25h after R Check if claim_type or claim_amount correlates Check if E exists between R and P

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 2 P to N Long Delay Find claims with P to N greater than 5 days Check resource adjuster Check if batch processing for example all notifications happen on Monday

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 3 A to C Fast Close Skipping Find claims where C happens shortly after A Check if E P N are missing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 4 E to N Fast Transition Find E to N less than 10 mins Check if P is missing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 5 Correlation with Adjusters Regions General query to see if anomalies cluster by adjuster Focus on A to C anomaly for adjusters

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The model says R to P STDEV is 1 hour This is suspiciously low The hypothesis should mention artificial scheduling or system generated timestamps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A to C avg 2 hours If the process is R A E P N C then A to C should be the sum of A to E E to P P to N N to C A to E is about 23 hours from the given values So A to C being 2 hours confirms massive skipping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: E to N is 5 mins P to N is 7 days If P exists then E to N should be much larger E to N being 5 mins confirms P is skipped or P to N is skipped
